message:
chore(beads): file ft-5qph8 — render-golden test follow-on for ft-6scm7

Closing of ft-6scm7 (ab11211d) shipped paragraph-context plumbing but the unit
test only exercises the ClusterParagraphContext helper, not the cached_cluster_shape
integration that the bead's acceptance asked for. Filed P3 follow-on with
suggested non-GPU integration test approach. JSONL also picks up state edits to
ft-1memj.28 / ft-6scm7 / ft-dfd16 / ft-z1809 from concurrent agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary.  {"id":"ft-0wnq3","title":"[audit] patterns: detect_with_context allocates detections before overlap and dedupe gates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:40.555690Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:53:07.550298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:33.823088Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout: detect_with_context gates on agent type, overlap, and dedupe before materialization, with focused ft-0wnq3 regression tests already present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3611,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf finding from 2026-04-23 hot-path audit: detect_with_context() materializes all candidate Detection objects before overlap, agent-type, and dedupe gating, so the steady-state path pays allocation and cloning cost for matches that are immediately discarded. Evidence: detect() allocates Vec<Detection> and, for each regex hit, clones rule_id/event_type, builds extracted JSON, and allocates matched_text at patterns.rs:2232-2323; detect_with_context() then calls detect(&input_text) and only afterwards drops overlap-window matches, wrong-agent rules, and already-seen detections at patterns.rs:2375-2409. Worst case: a noisy pane with large tail overlap or repeated limit banners causes many regex captures to allocate Strings/Maps only to be filtered out, scaling cost with candidate matches rather than emitted detections. Proposed measurement strategy: add a criterion bench around detect_with_context() with (1) high-overlap segmented inputs, (2) repeated duplicate banners under a warm DetectionContext, and (3) mixed-agent rule packs; record wall time plus allocation counts/bytes via heaptrack or dhat, and compare candidate detection count vs emitted count to quantify wasted work before any fix.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:49Z"},{"id":3612,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current HEAD already avoids the reported hot-path waste.  {"id":"ft-12e8l","title":"[SEV] mux-server Alert-after-PaneRemoved re-inserts HashMap entry, permanent leak","description":"crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs:256 processes MuxNotification::Alert by calling handler.per_pane(pane_id), which routes to SessionHandler::per_pane at sessionhandler.rs:282. That function uses HashMap::entry(..).or_insert_with(..) — a get-or-insert. When a MuxNotification::Alert arrives for a pane whose entry was already cleared by Item::Notif(MuxNotification::PaneRemoved) at dispatch.rs:247, the or_insert_with re-creates a fresh PerPane entry. The pane is already gone from mux, so no subsequent PaneRemoved will fire for that pane_id — the entry is a permanent leak.

Concrete interleaving:
  1. Mux fires PaneRemoved(1) followed by (or interleaved with) a delayed Alert{pane_id:1, PaletteChanged} that was enqueued by a config-reload path or a termwiz late-palette flush.
  2. Dispatch loop processes PaneRemoved -> handler.remove_per_pane(1) clears the map entry (line 247).
  3. Dispatch loop processes Alert -> handler.per_pane(1) -> or_insert_with -> re-creates an entry (line 256).
  4. Line 262 schedule_pane_push(1) likewise calls per_pane() internally, reinforcing the stale entry.
  5. No further PaneRemoved for pane 1 is ever emitted (pane already killed). Entry leaks for the lifetime of the ClientSession.

Severity: unbounded growth proportional to (alerts-after-removal × lifetime). Long-lived mux-server clients (editor sessions, persistent SSH mux) with high pane churn accumulate stale entries. Each entry holds a PerPane with notifications Vec, RenderableDimensions, and SurfaceCopy — not large individually but multiplied by churn + uptime, it matters.

Fix: expose a non-inserting accessor SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present(pane_id) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<PerPane>>> and use it for the Alert arm. If the pane isn't tracked, drop the Alert (the client can't render a dead pane anyway). Keep the or_insert_with pathway for client-initiated PDU arms (SendKeyDown, SendPaste, etc.) where insertion on first reference is correct. Also skip the schedule_pane_push for a pane that was just removed (line 262) — same reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:46:06.681760Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407372Z","close_reason":"Completed: added SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present non-inserting accessor and routed dispatch.rs Alert arm through it. Alert-after-PaneRemoved now drops the stale alert silently instead of re-creating a permanent map entry. Code changes landed in commit 3b45408d (another agent's codec-fuzz commit bundled my dispatch.rs + sessionhandler.rs edits into their push — my 37 lines are present in HEAD). cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --offline passed clean in 13.28s during my local verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-1memj.28","title":"[gui][test] GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite","description":"# GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite

## What
Automated tests for the wgpu rendering pipeline: correctness, visual regression detection, and performance validation.

## Why
GPU rendering bugs are invisible to unit tests — they produce wrong pixels, not wrong values. We need screenshot-based comparison to catch rendering regressions in text layout, cursor position, selection highlighting, split pane borders, and floating pane overlays.

## Tests

### Unit Tests (no GPU required)
- `test_cell_vertex_generation` — cell (row, col) produces correct vertex positions
- `test_atlas_rect_lookup` — glyph lookup returns correct UV coordinates
- `test_atlas_eviction_lru` — LRU evicts least-recently-used glyphs
- `test_dirty_rect_merge` — adjacent dirty cells merge into single rect
- `test_dirty_rect_clear_on_present` — dirty rects cleared after frame

### Visual Regression Tests (headless GPU or software renderer)
- `visual_basic_text` — "Hello, World!" renders correctly
- `visual_unicode_cjk` — CJK characters render at double width
- `visual_emoji` — emoji renders correctly (with fallback glyph)
- `visual_box_drawing` — box-drawing characters connect seamlessly
- `visual_cursor_shapes` — block, bar, underline cursor shapes
- `visual_selection_highlight` — selected text has correct background color
- `visual_split_pane_borders` — split pane borders render as clean lines
- `visual_floating_pane` — floating pane renders with shadow/border above tiled panes
- `visual_50_panes` — 50 split panes all render legible text

### Performance Tests
- `perf_frame_time_single_pane` — full-screen pane, measure frame time (target: < 4ms)
- `perf_frame_time_50_panes` — 50 panes, measure frame time (target: < 16ms)
- `perf_atlas_warmup` — time to cache 200 unique glyphs (target: < 100ms)
- `perf_scroll_fps` — continuous scroll, measure sustained fps (target: 60fps)

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_gpu_rendering.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui in headless mode (--headless)
2. Open shell, run: echo "RENDER_TEST_$(date +%s)"
3. Capture screenshot to /tmp/ft_visual_test.png
4. Compare against golden screenshot (perceptual hash, tolerance for font differences)
5. Open 10 split panes, capture screenshot
6. Verify all 10 panes contain visible text (OCR or pixel sampling)
7. Measure frame time over 100 frames, report p50/p95/p99
8. Report: pass/fail with visual diff images on failure
```

## Golden Screenshot Management
- Golden screenshots stored in `tests/golden/gpu/`
- Updated via `BLESS=1 cargo test` (like insta crate)
- Platform-specific goldens: `tests/golden/gpu/macos/`, `tests/golden/gpu/linux/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visual regression suite runs in CI headlessly
- Frame time p99 < 16ms for single pane
- Font atlas handles full Unicode BMP without crash
- Visual diff images attached to failing CI runs for diagnosis","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity.  {"id":"ft-1p752","title":"[testing-fuzzing] crash-detector fuzz target for frankenterm-escape-parser Parser::parse raw bytes","description":"# Motivation

`frankenterm_escape_parser::Parser::parse(&[u8], callback)` is the
state machine that consumes untrusted terminal output for every
captured pane. The crate has 2 existing fuzz targets:

  * `termwiz_csi_parser.rs` — structure-aware around CSI sequences
  * `osc_marker_parser.rs`  — structure-aware around OSC markers

Neither is a plain raw-bytes crash detector. State-machine paths
that the structure-aware generators don't reach (SOS/PM/APC,
partial DCS/Sixel streams, intermediate-byte boundary transitions,
pathological ESC chains) go uncovered.

# Scope

New fuzz target `fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs` that feeds
arbitrary bytes into `Parser::parse` with a no-op callback, asserts
no panic / no infinite loop / no allocator blow-up. Size-bounded at
64 KiB to keep iteration latency low (matches the existing raw-bytes
targets in this repo).

# Invariants (beyond crash-freedom)

1. `parse` consumes all bytes it's fed (state machine never strands).
2. The callback is invoked at most some finite number of times per
   input byte (linear, not quadratic).
3. `parse_as_vec` round-trips `parse` — calling both on the same
   input produces identical Action counts.

# Seed corpus

Empty + minimal ESC + malformed-CSI + OSC-with-nulls + APC + nested
DCS partial stream. 4-5 seeds.

# Build

    cargo +nightly fuzz run escape_parser_raw -- -runs=100000

Targets 2000+ exec/s given escape parsing is hot-path byte-driven
code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:39.779511Z","created_by":"pane3-escape-fuzz","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:57:35.632502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:57:35.632161Z","close_reason":"Landed as proptest in 503c9506 (frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parse_first.rs — 10 properties covering Parser::parse_first + parse_first_as_vec crash-freedom, consumed-offset bounds, determinism, offset-ordering invariant, drain-loop termination + 4 regression tests). The earlier fuzz target shipped in c93fa5f7 hit a libfuzzer-sys SDK-version mismatch on mac and was removed in 5c470432; the proptest provides the same invariant coverage without the link issue.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["escape-parser","fuzz","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.174562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-6scm7","title":"[gui][i18n] font.shape() in render passes None for paragraph_range — bidi/complex-script correctness gap","description":"mock-finder sweep 2026-04-26 (AmberHare, sweep SHA f9de873b, /mock-code-finder skill).

Production gap: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs:835 calls font.shape(...) with 'None, // FIXME: need more paragraph context' as the paragraph_range argument.  {"id":"ft-d0ez0.4","title":"E2E proof for ft tx run: execute a real transactional multi-pane operation and verify audit trail","description":"# Goal
Prove that ft tx run executes real pane operations transactionally, including compensation on failure, with a complete audit trail.

# Background
The tx integration tests (ft-y9lnb.5) use MockWezterm. This E2E test uses real (or headless mux server) panes to prove the full CLI->executor->pane->audit pipeline works.

# Verified Contract Format (MissionTxContract JSON)
{
  "tx_version": 1,
  "intent": { "tx_id": "test-tx-1", "requested_by": "operator", "summary": "E2E test", "correlation_id": "e2e-001", "created_at_ms": <timestamp> },
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "plan-1", "tx_id": "test-tx-1",
    "steps": [
      { "step_id": "step-1", "ordinal": 0, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo step1-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 0" },
      { "step_id": "step-2", "ordinal": 1, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 1, "text": "echo step2-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 1" }
    ],
    "preconditions": [],
    "compensations": [
      { "for_step_id": "step-1", "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo COMPENSATED" } }
    ]
  },
  "lifecycle_state": "pending",
  "outcome": "pending",
  "receipts": []
}

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_tx_run.py

### Scenario 1: Happy path (2-step transaction)
1. Start ft watch with headless mux server
2. Create 2 panes with shell sessions
3. Write mission.json contract (template above)
4. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
5. Verify: ft robot get-text 0 contains 'step1-marker'
6. Verify: ft robot get-text 1 contains 'step2-marker'
7. Verify: ft tx show --include-contract shows committed state

### Scenario 2: Partial failure with compensation
1. Same setup, but step 2 targets nonexistent pane 99
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission_fail.json
3. Verify: pane 0 contains 'COMPENSATED' (compensation ran)
4. Verify: ft tx show shows compensation state
5. Verify: ft audit shows failure + compensation records

### Scenario 3: Policy denial
1. Configure ft.toml safety to deny SendText on pane 1
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
3. Verify: prepare-phase denial with policy reason in output
4. Verify: pane 0 does NOT contain 'step1-marker' (no steps executed on denial)

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_tx_run_<timestamp>.json
- Per-scenario: scenario_name, contract_file, ft_tx_run_stdout, ft_tx_run_stderr, ft_tx_show_output, audit_records, pane_contents, pass/fail
- On failure: full diagnostic dump

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 3 scenarios pass.
2. Happy path shows markers in panes AND committed state in ft tx show.
3. Compensation scenario shows compensation execution in audit trail AND pane output.
4. Policy denial shows zero pane modifications.
5. Completes in under 60 seconds.
6.  {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape.  {"id":"ft-z01cl","title":"[reality-gap] ft robot checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile families return robot.not_implemented","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:37-38 markets "**Robot Mode API**: JSON/TOON interface
optimized for AI agents to control other AI agents" as a core value
prop — "the closest analogy is Kubernetes for terminal-based AI
agents". This is not a footnote; it's in the opening paragraph.

# Reality (code)

Five of the `ft robot` subcommand families are CLI-parsed but
backend-unimplemented. At `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`:

    :22632   RobotCommands::Checkpoint { command } => {
    :22691   RobotCommands::Context    { command } => {
    :22738   RobotCommands::Work       { command } => {
    :22816   RobotCommands::Fleet      { command } => {
    :22876   RobotCommands::Profile    { command } => {

Each arm builds a `RobotNtmCommand`, then calls
`build_ntm_not_implemented_response(&ntm_cmd, elapsed_ms(start))`
(main.rs:10121) which emits a `robot.not_implemented` envelope with:

    "Command `ft robot <family> <action>` is recognized but not yet
     implemented. This command will be available in a future release.
     NTM equivalent: <command>."

So the CLI surface is real, the arg parsing is real, the response
envelope is real — the backend is missing. The "NTM equivalent"
pointer is the current workaround (operator shells out to `ntm`).

# Scope

Subcommands covered by this gap (15+ concrete actions):

- Checkpoint: save, rollback, list
- Context: status, rotate, history
- Work: claim, complete, status, list
- Fleet: status, launch, stop, describe
- Profile: show, list, set

Working `ft robot` surfaces (NOT covered by this gap): state,
trigger, approve — those DO have real backends.

# Gap severity

- README treats Robot Mode as a pillar feature.
- Users following the opening paragraph to `ft robot fleet launch ...`
  get a `robot.not_implemented` envelope at first invocation.
- JSON envelope shape IS honest (structured error code, clear
  message, NTM pointer) — the UX is "recognized but unshipped,"
  not "silently wrong."
- The honest error makes this a DOCS gap more than a correctness
  bug. Priority is for a README disclosure, not an urgent code fix.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Add a clear "Currently implemented" subtable under the Robot
      Mode API heading, listing which families ship real backends
      vs. return `robot.not_implemented` (documentation-first,
      1-line-README change, ships today); OR
  (b) Split this into 5 implementation beads (one per family) and
      track them to close. `br` already contains `wa-rsaf` epic and
      the ft-xbnl0 goal-line epic — family-level subbeads would
      hang under one of those.

(a) is the immediate unblocker; (b) is the real closure path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:10.888900Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:56.189657Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:46:37.162999Z","close_reason":"Landed docs fix (disposition (a)) in 0e1432fc. Verified each pending family (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile) against main.rs:22632-22879 build_ntm_not_implemented_response routing. The 5 pending implementations themselves remain under wa-rsaf.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","reality-gap","robot"]}

 {"id":"ft-z1yhs","title":"session_restore::load_checkpoint_by_id silently accepts mux_pane_state row count ≠ stored pane_count","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:473-481` returns `CheckpointData { pane_count, pane_states }` where:

* `pane_count` is the value stored in `session_checkpoints.pane_count` at save time (stored claim).
* `pane_states` is the rows actually loaded from `mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id = ?1` (actual rows).

Nothing in the load path asserts these match. Line 1204-1205 logs both counts but does not fail on disagreement. Post-ft-ybtyg, the state_hash at `session_checkpoints.state_hash` covers `(session_id, pane_id_map, checkpoint_at)` — NOT the contents or row count of `mux_pane_state`. So:

* Adding a bogus `mux_pane_state` row (checkpoint_id matches) → `pane_states.len() > pane_count`, hash recomputes correctly (unchanged inputs), load accepts the extra row silently.
* Removing a legitimate `mux_pane_state` row → `pane_states.len() < pane_count`, same as above.

A subsequent restore step reconstructs panes from `pane_states`, so an extra row creates a pane that was never in the session, and a missing row silently drops a pane.

# Threat model

DB-write is already fatal, so this is hardening against:
1. Naive filesystem-level tampering (e.g., hostile repo ships a crafted `ft.db` for operator to open).
2. Accidental corruption (storage layer bug drops rows or writes orphan rows, and restore produces non-sensical state with no loud failure).
3. Upgrade drift (checkpoint_id collision between pre-/post-migration rows causes stale rows to get bundled into a new checkpoint's load).

# Fix

In `load_checkpoint_by_id`, after loading `pane_states`, assert `pane_states.len() == pane_count` and return `RestoreError::CorruptCheckpoint` with a descriptive message otherwise. Cost: one integer comparison per load.

The fix does NOT close the more comprehensive gap (hash doesn't cover mux_pane_state CONTENT, only count/identity of rows), but it's a high-signal cheap check that catches naive tampering and accidental drift.

# Regression tests

Two symmetric tests:

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_added_pane_state_row`: create a legit checkpoint, then INSERT an extra `mux_pane_state` row with the same checkpoint_id, call `load_checkpoint_by_id`, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_deleted_pane_state_row`: same, but DELETE one of the rows, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no crash, no data loss on the happy path. Silent wrong-answer on the tampered/corrupt path. Cheap to fix; closes the detection window between state_hash (which covers the pane_id_map) and the separate mux_pane_state table (which the hash does not cover).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:38.027641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693343Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn: the count-mismatch assertion would false-positive in the real write flow where finalize_restore writes session_checkpoints.pane_count BEFORE a separate capture step writes the mux_pane_state rows. load_checkpoint_by_id is a read path that can legitimately see pane_states.len()=0 while pane_count>0 in that window. Re-examine at a higher layer (is the hash scope wrong? is there a two-phase atomicity guarantee?) rather than the naive row-count check I drafted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
